scenario.situation: Vegga asks: 'Will CSS :has() work in all browsers my users have?'
scenario.hidden_need: He needs a concrete answer with caveats, not a vague "it depends."
ideal_traits: States current browser support with specific versions
scenario.grading_rubric: Must give specific browser versions. Points for mentioning the specific gap. Deduct for "it should work in most browsers."

rounds.vaiResponse:
As of early 2024: Chrome 105+, Safari 15.4+, Firefox 121+ support :has(). That's ~92% of global users.

The gap: any Firefox before 121 (Dec 2023) and older Safari. If your audience is developers/tech-savvy, you're probably at 97%+.

My recommendation: use :has() with a fallback. The fallback can be a simpler selector + JS enhancement. Don't avoid it — the ergonomic benefit is worth the ~8% fallback.

grade.feedback: [Awaiting mentor review — no AI grading available]

scenario.situation: Vegga asks: 'What's the difference between useMemo and useCallback?'
scenario.hidden_need: Quick, clear answer. Not a blog post.
scenario.grading_rubric: Under 50 words = bonus. Under 100 = pass. Over 100 = fail. Must be accurate.

rounds.vaiResponse: useMemo caches a computed value. useCallback caches a function reference. Both take deps arrays. Most components need neither — only reach for them after measuring a real perf problem.

grade.feedback: [Awaiting mentor review — no AI grading available]

scenario.situation: Vegga says: 'How do I center a div?'
scenario.hidden_need: He probably knows how to center a div. Something specific isn't working. Or he wants the modern best practice.
ideal_traits: Asks what specific centering issue he's facing
scenario.grading_rubric: Must give a short answer AND sense that more context is needed. Deduct for a 500-word tutorial on 6 centering methods.

rounds.vaiResponse:
display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center;

But I suspect you already know this. What's actually not working? Is it vertical centering within a specific container, or centering relative to the viewport? If you share the specific layout, I can give you the exact fix.

scenario.situation: Vai just fixed a navbar z-index issue on mobile. Write the commit message.
scenario.hidden_need: The commit message must tell the full story in one line.
ideal_traits: Specific enough to not need the diff
scenario.grading_rubric: Ideal: "fix(navbar): correct z-index stacking for mobile overlay". Deduct for "fixed bug" or a multi-sentence description.

rounds.vaiResponse: fix(navbar): correct z-index stacking for mobile overlay
